data.title: Visa Moves Billions, Makes Billions — And Raises Outlook
data.description: Visa, Inc. (NYSE: V) saw a surge in premarket trading as the company unveiled its first-quarter results exceeding expectations. The earnings per share stood at $3.31, beating the projected $3.10, with revenue hitting $11.23 billion compared to the estimated $10.74 billion. Visa experienced a significant 17% year-over-year increase in net revenue, marking its speediest growth rate since 2022. Payments volume also rose by 9% to $3.7 trillion, while total transactions increased by 9% to 66 billion. The positive outcomes have led Visa to revise its full-year outlook upwards.

data.title: Jack Daniel's Maker Brown-Forman Stock Drops Nearly 6% In Pre-Market: What's Going On?
data.description: Brown-Forman (NYSE: BF) saw a 5.88% decrease in premarket trading as French spirits giant Pernod Ricard S.A. (OTC: PRNDY) and the Jack Daniel’s whiskey manufacturer mutually decided to end merger discussions. Pernod Ricard expressed confidence in its strategy and team, prioritizing long-term stakeholder value, while Brown-Forman stated its objectives to enhance global presence, fortify its brands, and streamline operations. Reports from the Wall Street Journal highlighted disagreements over the deal’s structure and financial terms, resulting in the inability of both parties to agree on merger conditions. Notably, both Pernod and Brown-Forman have notable family ownership, with any Brown-Forman deal necessitating approval from the majority-holding Brown family. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.
